description: A light Jell-O salad, great for summer barbecues or with Christmas dinner!
ingredients: 1 (3 ounce) package lime flavored Jell-O® mix
1 (8 ounce) container frozen whipped topping, thawed
directions: Gather all ingredients.
In a small saucepan, heat 1 cup reserved pear juice to boiling. Pour over lime gelatin in a small bowl; stir until dissolved. Set aside.
In a large bowl, beat cream cheese with milk until smooth. Blend in gelatin mixture. Chill in the refrigerator until partially thickened, about 20 to 30 minutes.
In a small bowl, mash pears until smooth. Fold pears and whipped topping into gelatin mixture. Pour into a mold. Chill until set, about 2 to 3 hours.
Dip mold in warm water for approximately 5 seconds. Invert onto a serving plate

description: Sweet, salty, cool and crunchy, this salad is a great addition to any family buffet.
ingredients: 1 (3 ounce) package lemon flavored Jell-O® mix
1 (3 ounce) package lime flavored Jell-O® mix
½ cup chopped pimento-stuffed green olives
directions: Gather the ingredients.

In a large bowl, stir together the lemon gelatin mix, lime gelatin mix, and boiling water until gelatin has dissolved. Stir in the apple cider vinegar, cover, and refrigerate for about 1 hour to thicken.
When the gelatin is thick but not set, stir in the celery, olives, and carrot so they are evenly dispersed throughout.
Pour into a 1 quart mold and refrigerate until firm, at least 3 hours. To serve, unmold onto a plate.

description: A refreshing chilled salad with orange gelatin, pineapple, buttermilk, and whipped topping.
ingredients: 1 (8 ounce) container frozen whipped topping, thawed
directions: Mix pineapple and sugar together in a medium saucepan, and bring to a low boil, stirring occasionally. Stir in gelatin until completely dissolved, remove from heat, and cool to room temperature. Add buttermilk, and refrigerate 1 hour, or until partially set.
Gently fold in whipped topping. Pour mixture into a lightly oiled gelatin mold. Refrigerate 8 hours, or until completely set.

description: I serve this creamy wheat and pineapple salad at Thanksgiving and Christmas dinner. It's a family favorite!
ingredients: 1 (15.25 ounce) can crushed pineapple in juice, drained
1 (3.4 ounce) package instant vanilla pudding mix
1 (8 ounce) tub frozen whipped topping, thawed
directions: Soak wheat berries in a bowl of water for at least 5 hours or overnight. Drain and place in a slow cooker with enough water to cover by one inch. Cover, and cook on Low setting for 5 to 6 hours, until tender. Add more water if needed. When tender, drain, and rinse under cold water to chill.

In a large bowl, stir together the cream cheese, pineapple and lemon juice. Stir in the dry pudding mix until smooth. Stir in the wheat berries until evenly coated. Fold in whipped topping just before serving. Refrigerate any leftovers.

description: This ambrosia salad with fruit cocktail is a long-time family favorite for holidays or weekend BBQs.
ingredients: 1 (16 ounce) can sliced peaches in juice (Optional)
1 (16 ounce) can pineapple chunks in juice
1 (12 ounce) can mandarin oranges in juice
1 (12 ounce) container frozen whipped topping (such as Cool Whip®), thawed
directions: Empty fruit cocktail, peaches, pineapple, and oranges out into a strainer set over a large bowl to drain.
Meanwhile, beat cream cheese in a bowl, adding milk until mixture is smooth and creamy. Mix in 3/4 of the whipped topping and 1/4 cup of the drained juice; blend well.

Dice peaches and fold them into the creamy mixture. Add all the drained fruits and coconut, reserving 5 or 6 mandarins, and stir to coat. Smooth over the top and frost with remaining whipped topping. Decorate top with reserved mandarins. Refrigerate until flavors combine.

Meanwhile, beat cream cheese in a bowl, adding milk until mixture is smooth and creamy. Mix in 3/4 of the whipped topping and 1/4 cup of the drained juice; blend well.

description: This is my most requested recipe! It's a wonderful accompaniment to your Thanksgiving or Christmas menu.
ingredients: 1 (6 ounce) package cherry-flavored gelatin mix (such as Jell-O®)
2 (14.5 ounce) cans whole berry cranberry sauce
directions: Dissolve cherry gelatin mix in boiling water in a bowl; stir cranberry sauce and oranges into gelatin. Refrigerate until mixture is thickened to the consistency of egg whites, about 45 minutes. Mix apple and walnuts into gelatin, transfer to a serving dish or mold, and chill until firm, about 2 more hours.

description: Very easy to make Jell-O mold. I have made it with different flavors (colors) and it is always a crowd pleaser!
ingredients: 1 (12 ounce) container frozen whipped topping, thawed
1 (6 ounce) package strawberry-flavored gelatin (such as Jell-O)
1 (8 ounce) can crushed pineapple with juice
directions: Gather the ingredients.
Place whipped topping in a large bowl and stir until thawed and smooth.
Add gelatin and mix well.
Add pineapple with juice, walnuts, and marshmallows.
Mix together and transfer into a mold of your choice. Refrigerate for 8 hours to overnight before unmolding.

description: A creamy Bing cherry gelatin salad.
ingredients: 1 (16.5 ounce) can pitted Bing cherries, drained, juice reserved
1 (15 ounce) can crushed pineapple, drained with juice reserved
directions: In a sauce pan, combine reserved cherry juice and pineapple juice to equal 1 cup. Bring to boil, add gelatin and whisk together. Remove from heat and add cream cheese and blend together until smooth.
Add cola and beat until mixture becomes fluffy. Fold in cherries, pineapple, and nuts. Pour into molded container, chill 6 to 8 hours or until firm.

description: This savory Jell-O salad is made with cabbage, chopped ham, mayonnaise, and spicy brown mustard.
ingredients: 1 (3 ounce) package lemon-flavored gelatin mix (such as Jell-O®)
1 (8 ounce) can crushed pineapple in juice, drained and juice reserved
½ cup cold water, or as needed
2 teaspoons spicy brown mustard, or to taste
directions: Dissolve lemon gelatin in boiling water in a large bowl. Pour reserved pineapple juice and white wine vinegar into a measuring cup; add enough cold water to measure 1 cup. Stir water-juice mixture into gelatin mixture; refrigerate until cold and beginning to set, about 30 minutes.

Stir mayonnaise and spicy brown mustard together in a bowl; whisk mayonnaise mixture into cooled lemon gelatin mixture until smooth. Gently fold cabbage, ham, celery, and reserved pineapple into gelatin. Transfer mixture into a ring-shaped gelatin mold.
Chill in the refrigerator until firmly set, about 4 hours.
To serve, dip the gelatin mold into hot water to loosen the sides; place a plate on top of the mold, invert, and gently lift the mold from salad.

description: Orange cream-flavored gelatin salad with pineapple and mandarin oranges. This orange tapioca salad is a family favorite in the summer.
ingredients: 1 (3 ounce) package orange flavored Jell-O® mix
1 (3.4 ounce) package instant vanilla pudding mix
1 (3 ounce) package instant tapioca pudding mix
1 (8 ounce) tub frozen whipped topping, thawed
directions: Bring water to a boil in a large saucepan. Whisk in orange gelatin mix, vanilla pudding mix, and tapioca pudding mix. Return to a boil and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and allow to cool completely.
Fold in mandarin oranges, crushed pineapple, and whipped topping; transfer to a serving bowl.
Chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours. Use a spoon to fluff top before serving.

description: Old-fashioned carrot Jell-O salad made with lemon and pineapple. Grandma didn't specify the amount of carrots. You probably can't use too much!
ingredients: 1 (6 ounce) package lemon flavored Jell-O® mix
1 (20 ounce) can crushed pineapple, drained with juice reserved
directions: Gather the ingredients.
In a large bowl, prepare lemon gelatin according to package directions using reserved pineapple juice in place of some of the water. Refrigerate until thickened, about 1 hour.
When gelatin has thickened, stir in pineapple and shredded carrot.
Cover and refrigerate until set, at least 4 hours.

description: This is a fruit salad or dessert that my family has always called sawdust salad because of the toasted coconut on top.
ingredients: 1 (3 ounce) package lemon flavored Jell-O® mix
1 (3 ounce) package orange flavored Jell-O® mix
directions: Gather the ingredients.
In a large bowl, combine lemon and orange gelatin powders and boiling water. Stir until powders are dissolved, then stir in drained pineapple and marshmallows. Refrigerate until firm.

While gelatin mixture is chilling, whisk together pineapple juice, sugar, eggs, and flour in a medium saucepan and cook over medium heat until thick. Stir constantly and do not allow mixture to scorch.
Remove from heat and let cool before spreading over the set gelatin mixture.
Toss sliced bananas with lemon juice to prevent browning. Drain, then layer sliced bananas over custard mixture.
Prepare whipped topping mix according to package directions, then beat in cream cheese until smooth.
Spread mixture over bananas. Sprinkle with toasted coconut.
Chill salad for 2 to 4 hours before serving.

description: This orange vinaigrette is made with fresh orange juice for a low-fat and delicious salad dressing that's easy to throw together in a few minutes.
directions: Gather the ingredients.
Combine orange juice, vinegar, mustard, honey, and pepper in a small jar with a lid. Cover and vigorously shake until combined.
Store in the refrigerator for up to 1 week. Shake well before serving.
Enjoy!

description: This raspberry vinaigrette is a super quick-and-easy salad dressing to use up those extra raspberries in the summer. Adjust the amount of vinegar to suit your taste.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon white sugar or to taste
⅔ cup balsamic vinegar, or to taste
directions: Mix raspberries and sugar together in a bowl; set aside until mixture is juicy, about 10 minutes. Mash raspberries using a fork until liquefied.
Pour raspberry mixture into a clean jar with a lid; add vinegar, oil, honey, and salt. Cover the jar with a lid and shake until dressing is mixed well. Store in the refrigerator.

recipe_title: Strawberry and Spinach Salad with Honey Balsamic Vinaigrette
description: This strawberry spinach salad with balsamic is a great easy summer dish with many options for mixing and matching. Plus, the salad looks pretty fancy. You can throw in any summer fruit or berry with this mix. Blueberries and raspberries are a good choice, as are blackberries later in the season. The nuts are optional, but pecans or roasted almonds add a little crunch and more protein!
ingredients: salt and ground black pepper to taste
directions: Combine spinach, strawberries, Gorgonzola cheese, and pecans in a large bowl.
Stir together balsamic vinegar and honey in a medium bowl; slowly stream in olive oil while whisking continuously. Season with salt and pepper.
Drizzle dressing over spinach mixture just before serving.

directions: Combine lemon juice, zest, garlic, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper in a medium bowl. Slowly whisk in olive oil until thickened. Whisk in sour cream until well combined. Transfer dressing to a sealable container.
Chill dressing in the refrigerator, 15 minutes to overnight.

description: This is a light and lovely salad dressing that lends itself well to a simple and elegant salad. I love to use it on a salad of butter lettuce, arugula, goat cheese, and caramelized pecans - simply delicious!
ingredients: 1 ripe pear - peeled, cored, and chopped
directions: Blend the pear, white wine, garlic, Dijon mustard, white balsamic vinegar, black pepper, and sea salt in a blender until well combined; drizzle the olive oil into the mixture in a thin, steady stream while continuing to blend. Blend a few seconds longer until the salad dressing is thick and creamy.
ingredient_text: ripe pear - peeled cored chopped ½ white wine clove garlic chopped dijon mustard ¼ white balsamic vinegar ground black pepper ¼ sea salt ½ olive oil
directions_text: blend the pear, white wine, garlic, dijon mustard, white balsamic vinegar, black pepper, and sea salt in a blender until well combined; drizzle the olive oil into the mixture in a thin, steady stream while continuing to blend. blend a few seconds longer until the salad dressing is thick and creamy.

recipe_title: Orange, Walnut, Gorgonzola and Mixed Greens Salad with Fresh Citrus Vinaigrette
description: A tasty summery salad made with fresh oranges, red onions, glazed walnuts, and Gorgonzola cheese, then topped with a homemade citrus vinaigrette! Easy and delicious!
ingredients: 10 ounces mixed salad greens with arugula
2 large navel oranges, peeled and sectioned
directions: Place the walnuts in a skillet over medium heat. Cook 5 minutes, stirring constantly, until lightly browned.
In a large bowl, toss the toasted walnuts, salad greens, oranges, and red onion.
In a large jar with a lid, mix the olive oil, vegetable oil, orange juice, sugar, vinegar, mustard, oregano, and pepper. Seal jar, and shake to mix.
Divide the salad greens mixture into individual servings. To serve, sprinkle with Gorgonzola cheese, and drizzle with the dressing mixture.

description: An easy salad to make with a bag of salad greens, pineapple, bacon bits, nuts and toasted coconut. Use fresh pineapple, if you can, and substitute toasted almonds for the macadamia nuts, if desired.
ingredients: freshly ground black pepper to taste
½ cup chopped and toasted macadamia nuts
directions: Place bacon in a large, deep skillet. Cook over medium-high heat until evenly browned, about 10 minutes. Drain, crumble, and set aside.
Combine pineapple juice, red wine vinegar, oil, pepper and salt in a lidded jar or cruet. Cover and shake well.
Toss lettuce, pineapple, macadamia nuts, green onions and bacon together in a large bowl. Pour dressing over salad and toss to coat. Garnish with toasted coconut.

Toss lettuce, pineapple, macadamia nuts, green onions and bacon together in a large bowl. Pour dressing over salad and toss to coat. Garnish with toasted coconut.

description: Yummy vinaigrette dressing with a nice richness thanks to some balsamic vinegar and fresh blueberries! I prefer a little less oil for a thicker dressing and my husband prefers a little more oil for a thinner dressing.
directions: Combine oil, blueberries, white vinegar, balsamic vinegar, and honey together in a food processor; blend until smooth.
ingredient_text: ¾ vegetable oil ½ fresh blueberries white vinegar balsamic vinegar honey
directions_text: combine oil, blueberries, white vinegar, balsamic vinegar, and honey together in a food processor; blend until smooth.

description: This recipe for green salad with cranberry vinaigrette is especially pretty to serve during the Christmas holidays.
directions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
Arrange almonds in a single layer on a baking sheet. Toast in the preheated oven until nuts begin to brown, about 5 minutes.
Combine oil, cranberries, vinegar, water, Dijon mustard, garlic, salt, and black pepper in a blender or the bowl of a food processor; blend until vinaigrette is smooth.
Toss mixed greens, almonds, onion, and blue cheese with vinaigrette in a large bowl until evenly coated.
ingredient_text: sliced almonds ⅓ olive oil ¼ fresh cranberries red wine vinegar water dijon mustard ½ minced garlic ½ salt ½ ground black pepper pound mixed salad greens ½ red onion thinly sliced ounces crumbled blue cheese

Toss mixed greens, almonds, onion, and blue cheese with vinaigrette in a large bowl until evenly coated.

description: This tasteful tahini salad dressing is full of Middle Eastern flavors; lemon, garlic, red wine vinegar, and ginger give it a kick! It's easy to make in a blender and very versatile — add a bit less tamari, more lemon juice, and a pinch of ground mint if you prefer. People always ask me for this salad dressing recipe.
ingredients: ¼ cup tamari (dark soy sauce) or to taste
freshly ground black pepper to taste
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Combine tahini, olive oil, water, tamari, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, ginger, garlic, and black pepper in a blender; blend until smooth.
ingredient_text: ½ tahini paste ½ olive oil ½ water ¼ tamari dark soy sauce taste red wine vinegar lemon juice minced fresh ginger cloves garlic minced freshly ground black pepper taste
directions_text: gather all ingredients. combine tahini, olive oil, water, tamari, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, ginger, garlic, and black pepper in a blender; blend until smooth.

description: This is a delicious, tangy vinaigrette. It's so versatile, it can be used for mixed greens or taco salads. Enjoy! Serve immediately or keep in the fridge for 4 to 5 days. I prefer white wine vinegar in this recipe, as opposed to regular white vinegar. You can also use Dijon mustard if you prefer to spicy brown.
directions: Blend the lime juice, vinegar, and cilantro together in a blender until smooth. Add the brown sugar, garlic, and salt; blend again until smooth. Spoon the mustard into the mixture. Turn the blender on and slowly pour the olive oil into the dressing mixture in a thin stream; blend until thoroughly combined.
ingredient_text: ¼ lime juice white vinegar ½ bunch cilantro chopped brown sugar clove garlic minced ¼ salt ¾ spicy brown mustard ¾ light olive oil
directions_text: blend the lime juice, vinegar, and cilantro together in a blender until smooth. add the brown sugar, garlic, and salt; blend again until smooth. spoon the mustard into the mixture. turn the blender on and slowly pour the olive oil into the dressing mixture in a thin stream; blend until thoroughly combined.

Turn the blender on and slowly pour the olive oil into the dressing mixture in a thin stream; blend until thoroughly combined.

description: When Meyer lemons are in season, typically between November and March, be sure to snag some to make this Meyer lemon vinaigrette. Meyer lemons are much less tart and acidic than traditional lemons; they're like a cross between a lemon and a mandarin. They're also sweeter, so no additional sugar is needed. I love this on kale salad, but also drizzled over roasted veggies or grilled salmon or trout.
directions: Combine olive oil, Meyer lemon juice, balsamic vinegar, Parmesan cheese, garlic, zest, salt, and pepper in a small blender cup. Blend until dressing is smooth, 30 to 45 seconds.
ingredient_text: ¼ extra-virgin olive oil meyer lemon juice white balsamic vinegar freshly grated parmesan cheese small garlic clove ½ meyer lemon zest ¼ salt ⅛ ground black pepper
directions_text: combine olive oil, meyer lemon juice, balsamic vinegar, parmesan cheese, garlic, zest, salt, and pepper in a small blender cup. blend until dressing is smooth, 30 to 45 seconds.

directions_raw: Combine olive oil, Meyer lemon juice, balsamic vinegar, Parmesan cheese, garlic, zest, salt, and pepper in a small blender cup. Blend until dressing is smooth, 30 to 45 seconds.

recipe_title: Apple Walnut Salad with Cranberry Vinaigrette
description: This is a great salad to serve for any autumn meal.
ingredients: 10 cups mixed salad greens, rinsed and dried
2 Red Delicious apples, cored and thinly sliced
directions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Spread the walnuts out on a baking sheet in a single layer. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes in the preheated oven, or until lightly toasted.
In a food processor, combine the cranberries, vinegar, onion, sugar, and mustard. Puree until smooth; gradually add oil, and season with salt and pepper.
In a salad bowl, toss together the greens, apples, and enough of the cranberry mixture to coat. Sprinkle with walnuts, and serve.

recipe_title: Cucumber Tomato Salad with Zucchini and Black Olives in Lemon Balsamic Vinaigrette
description: This zucchini salad is a deliciously light and fresh-tasting twist on cucumber tomato salad. It takes me to the water's edge and begs to be served with fresh seafood. Substitute Greek Kalamata olives for black olives if desired.
ingredients: 1 ¼ teaspoons kosher salt, or to taste
¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
directions: Mix cucumbers, zucchini, red onion, tomatoes, black olives, basil, and thyme together in a large salad bowl.

In a separate bowl, whisk red wine vinegar, balsamic vinegar, lemon zest, lemon juice, kosher salt, sugar, and white pepper together until thoroughly combined. Pour olive oil slowly into dressing mixture, whisking to combine. Pour dressing over salad and serve.
ingredient_text: large cucumbers diced zucchini diced ½ red onion thinly sliced large tomatoes diced chopped black olives chopped fresh basil fresh thyme leaves red wine vinegar balsamic vinegar ½ lemon zest ½ lemon juiced ¼ kosher salt taste ½ white sugar ¼ freshly ground black pepper ½ extra-virgin olive oil
directions_text: mix cucumbers, zucchini, red onion, tomatoes, black olives, basil, and thyme together in a large salad bowl. in a separate bowl, whisk red wine vinegar, balsamic vinegar, lemon zest, lemon juice, kosher salt, sugar, and white pepper together until thoroughly combined. pour olive oil slowly into dressing mixture, whisking to combine. pour dressing over salad and serve.

In a separate bowl, whisk red wine vinegar, balsamic vinegar, lemon zest, lemon juice, kosher salt, sugar, and white pepper together until thoroughly combined. Pour olive oil slowly into dressing mixture, whisking to combine. Pour dressing over salad and serve.

description: This raspberry balsamic dressing tastes fantastic on a salad with fruit (apples are my favorite) or even as a bread dip! I was tired of trying bottle after bottle of dressing and not finding anything close to my favorite restaurant-inspired salad until I made this recipe.
directions: Combine oil, raspberry balsamic vinegar, balsamic vinegar, brown sugar, garlic, and mustard in a sealable jar. Seal the jar and shake vigorously until dressing is well-combined. Refrigerate until flavors combine, at least 1 hour.
ingredient_text: ¼ vegetable oil raspberry balsamic vinegar balsamic vinegar brown sugar clove garlic minced ½ honey dijon mustard

description: This citrus salad dressing is delightful and tastes great in any season. Store unused dressing in the refrigerator.
directions: Whisk together vinegar, orange juice, oil, cilantro, mustard, garlic, and honey in a bowl.
ingredient_text: ⅔ rice wine vinegar ⅓ orange juice olive oil chopped fresh cilantro dijon mustard minced garlic honey
directions_text: whisk together vinegar, orange juice, oil, cilantro, mustard, garlic, and honey in a bowl.

description: This recipe uses cultured coconut milk as a substitution for buttermilk and mayonnaise in a dill-flavored, creamy dairy-free salad dressing. Enjoy! I found an alternative to dairy-based yogurt. Cultured coconut milk is not as thick as yogurt, but it works as a substitute for buttermilk and mayonnaise in a traditional ranch-style salad dressing or dip. It's not a perfect sub, but for anyone who is avoiding dairy, it's worth a try. Look for this product in the yogurt section of your grocer. It's also great in smoothies!
ingredients: salt and ground black pepper to taste
directions: Whisk coconut milk, parsley, lemon juice, garlic powder, dill, onion powder, mustard, salt, and black pepper together in a bowl until smooth and creamy. Refrigerate until flavors blend, at least 30 minutes.
ingredient_text: cultured coconut milk ½ dried parsley ½ lemon juice garlic powder dill ½ onion powder ½ dijon mustard salt ground black pepper taste
directions_text: whisk coconut milk, parsley, lemon juice, garlic powder, dill, onion powder, mustard, salt, and black pepper together in a bowl until smooth and creamy. refrigerate until flavors blend, at least 30 minutes.

directions_raw: Whisk coconut milk, parsley, lemon juice, garlic powder, dill, onion powder, mustard, salt, and black pepper together in a bowl until smooth and creamy. Refrigerate until flavors blend, at least 30 minutes.

description: The sweet tang from blood oranges makes a beautiful vinaigrette. Toss it over your favorite greens, some fresh slices of blood orange, and a few candied nuts for an amazing salad.
ingredients: salt and ground black pepper to taste
directions: Combine olive oil, blood orange juice, red wine vinegar, honey, mustard, salt, and pepper in a blender. Process until well combined and smooth.

description: Here's a smooth and tasty alternative to regular Caesar dressing. This Caesar vinaigrette dressing recipe has stood the test of time in my kitchen and is requested often. It tastes best on romaine tossed with spring mix lettuce. The dressing will keep up to two weeks in the refrigerator in an airtight container.
ingredients: ½ cup freshly shredded Parmesan cheese
directions: Stir white wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, balsamic vinegar, lemon zest, sugar, Italian seasoning, salt, and black pepper together in a bowl.
Gradually whisk in olive oil to make an emulsion or thickened vinaigrette mixture. Stir in garlic. Add Parmesan cheese just before serving.
ingredient_text: white wine vinegar dijon mustard balsamic vinegar lemon zest white sugar ½ italian seasoning ¼ salt ⅛ ground black pepper ⅔ olive oil clove garlic minced ½ freshly shredded parmesan cheese

description: This orange dressing for spinach salad can be made with all raw ingredients. It's great on a spinach salad with oranges or other lettuce-and-fruit salads.

directions: Combine parsley, orange juice, honey, olive oil, white wine vinegar, grated orange peel, salt, and cayenne pepper in a blender, and pulse until blended. Refrigerate mixture 4 hours for the best flavor. Shake gently just before serving.

description: A delicious, versatile creamy vinaigrette dressing for pasta or green salads. This is easily made in advance for use just before serving.
ingredients: ¼ cup rice wine vinegar or lemon juice
salt and freshly ground black pepper
directions: Measure vinegar (or lemon juice) and mustard (or mayonnaise) into a 2-cup measuring cup. With a small whisk, mix in garlic, a big pinch of salt, and a few grinds of pepper. Measure oil in another cup. Slowly whisk oil into mixture, first in droplets, then in a slow, steady stream to make an emulsified vinaigrette.

description: This tangy pomegranate dressing goes great on any salad. It's addictive!
directions: Place olive oil, pomegranate juice, rice vinegar, white sugar, shallots, garlic, white pepper, and salt in a blender. Blend until smooth, about 2 minutes.
ingredient_text: olive oil pomegranate juice rice vinegar ¼ white sugar chopped shallots ¼ chopped garlic ground white pepper kosher salt taste
directions_text: place olive oil, pomegranate juice, rice vinegar, white sugar, shallots, garlic, white pepper, and salt in a blender. blend until smooth, about 2 minutes.
combined_text: yummy pomegranate vinaigrette dressing fruit salads fruit dressing 1 cup olive oil 1 cup pomegranate juice 1 cup rice vinegar ¼ cup white sugar 2 tablespoons chopped shallots 1 ¼ teaspoons chopped garlic 1 teaspoon ground white pepper kosher salt to taste place olive oil, pomegranate juice, rice vinegar, white sugar, shallots, garlic, white pepper, and salt in a blender. blend until smooth, about 2 minutes.
directions_raw: Place olive oil, pomegranate juice, rice vinegar, white sugar, shallots, garlic, white pepper, and salt in a blender. Blend until smooth, about 2 minutes.

description: Raspberry vinaigrette with jam is a very easy, flavor-filled dressing that goes with any kind of salad. Only three ingredients! I like to use it with romaine, feta, toasted pecans, bacon, and red onion.
directions: Combine olive oil, rice vinegar, and raspberry jam in the container of a blender. Process until smooth. Store in a jar in the refrigerator.
ingredient_text: ¼ olive oil seasoned rice vinegar ounce jar seedless raspberry jam
directions_text: combine olive oil, rice vinegar, and raspberry jam in the container of a blender. process until smooth. store in a jar in the refrigerator.
combined_text: red raspberry vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing ¼ cup olive oil 1 cup seasoned rice vinegar 1 (10 ounce) jar seedless raspberry jam combine olive oil, rice vinegar, and raspberry jam in the container of a blender. process until smooth. store in a jar in the refrigerator.
directions_raw: Combine olive oil, rice vinegar, and raspberry jam in the container of a blender. Process until smooth. Store in a jar in the refrigerator.

description: This dressing is lovely yellow color, and we use it on a mixed green salad (mixed Romaine and European greens), tossed with small broccoli and cauliflower florets, raisins or sweetened dried cranberries, and shredded Cheddar or crumbled Gorgonzola cheese. The sweet-tart zestiness of the dressing perks up the salad! The yield depends on how much salad dressing you prefer. For us, this recipe makes enough dressing for about 4 large salads.
directions: In a jar with a lid, mix the olive oil, orange juice, lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, honey, basil, and vinegar. Seal and shake well. Chill 2 hours in the refrigerator. Strain basil before serving.
ingredient_text: ¼ olive oil orange juice lemon juice ¼ lemon zested ½ salt honey ⅛ chopped fresh basil white wine vinegar
directions_text: in a jar with a lid, mix the olive oil, orange juice, lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, honey, basil, and vinegar. seal and shake well. chill 2 hours in the refrigerator. strain basil before serving.
combined_text: citrus-basil vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing ¼ cup olive oil 2 tablespoons orange juice 1 tablespoon lemon juice ¼ lemon, zested ½ teaspoon salt 1 tablespoon honey ⅛ cup chopped fresh basil 1 teaspoon white wine vinegar in a jar with a lid, mix the olive oil, orange juice, lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, honey, basil, and vinegar. seal and shake well. chill 2 hours in the refrigerator. strain basil before serving.
directions_raw: In a jar with a lid, mix the olive oil, orange juice, lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, honey, basil, and vinegar. Seal and shake well. Chill 2 hours in the refrigerator. Strain basil before serving.

description: The cranberries add that perfect sweet-tart flavor to this dressing. You can substitute dried cherries or raspberries for the cranberries.
directions: Whisk vinegar, cranberries, sugar, mustard, salt, garlic, and pepper together in a bowl until the sugar and salt are completely dissolved. Stream oil into the mixture while whisking continually; continue whisking until the oil emulsifies into the vinegar.
ingredient_text: ¼ balsamic vinegar finely chopped dried cranberries white sugar dijon mustard ½ salt clove garlic peeled smashed freshly ground black pepper ½ extra-virgin olive oil
directions_text: whisk vinegar, cranberries, sugar, mustard, salt, garlic, and pepper together in a bowl until the sugar and salt are completely dissolved. stream oil into the mixture while whisking continually; continue whisking until the oil emulsifies into the vinegar.
combined_text: cranberry balsamic vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing ¼ cup balsamic vinegar 1 tablespoon finely chopped dried cranberries 1 teaspoon white sugar 1 teaspoon dijon mustard ½ teaspoon salt 1 clove garlic, peeled and smashed freshly ground black pepper ½ cup extra-virgin olive oil whisk vinegar, cranberries, sugar, mustard, salt, garlic, and pepper together in a bowl until the sugar and salt are completely dissolved. stream oil into the mixture while whisking continually; continue whisking until the oil emulsifies into the vinegar.
directions_raw: Whisk vinegar, cranberries, sugar, mustard, salt, garlic, and pepper together in a bowl until the sugar and salt are completely dissolved. Stream oil into the mixture while whisking continually; continue whisking until the oil emulsifies into the vinegar.

description: This chili-lime vinaigrette is great with green salads topped with broiled salmon or chicken. It also makes a wonderful marinade for meat and tofu. If you don't want it as spicy, you can cut back on the red pepper flakes, but we love it just the way it is.
directions: Whisk lime juice, soy sauce, brown sugar, oil, garlic, and pepper flakes together in a bowl until creamy and smooth. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate until flavors have blended, 8 hours to overnight.
ingredient_text: ¾ lime juice ¾ soy sauce packed brown sugar ¾ olive oil ¼ minced garlic ¼ red pepper flakes
directions_text: whisk lime juice, soy sauce, brown sugar, oil, garlic, and pepper flakes together in a bowl until creamy and smooth. cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate until flavors have blended, 8 hours to overnight.
combined_text: chili lime vinaigrette dressing fruit salads fruit dressing 3 ¾ cups lime juice 1 ¾ cups soy sauce 1 cup packed brown sugar ¾ cup olive oil ¼ cup minced garlic ¼ cup red pepper flakes whisk lime juice, soy sauce, brown sugar, oil, garlic, and pepper flakes together in a bowl until creamy and smooth. cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate until flavors have blended, 8 hours to overnight.
directions_raw: Whisk lime juice, soy sauce, brown sugar, oil, garlic, and pepper flakes together in a bowl until creamy and smooth. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate until flavors have blended, 8 hours to overnight.

description: Vibrant and fruity, this orange cumin vinaigrette recipe requires no whisking, drizzling, or machinery of any kind — just a jar with a lid. This "make and shake" method should only be used if you're going to eat your salad right away.
ingredients: ⅓ cup olive oil, or to taste
1 teaspoon ground cumin, or to taste
¼ teaspoon red pepper flakes, or to taste
1 pinch ground black pepper, or to taste
directions: Place olive oil, rice wine vinegar, orange zest, orange juice, Dijon mustard, cumin, and red pepper flakes into a jar with a screw top; season with salt and black pepper.
Cover jar; shake vigorously to form a temporary emulsion, about 2 minutes.
ingredient_text: ⅓ olive oil taste ¼ rice wine vinegar ½ grated orange zest fresh orange juice ½ dijon mustard ground cumin taste ¼ red pepper flakes taste pinch salt taste pinch ground black pepper taste
directions_text: place olive oil, rice wine vinegar, orange zest, orange juice, dijon mustard, cumin, and red pepper flakes into a jar with a screw top; season with salt and black pepper. cover jar; shake vigorously to form a temporary emulsion, about 2 minutes.
combined_text: orange cumin vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing ⅓ cup olive oil, or to taste ¼ cup rice wine vinegar 1 ½ teaspoons grated orange zest 1 tablespoon fresh orange juice 1 ½ teaspoons dijon mustard 1 teaspoon ground cumin, or to taste ¼ teaspoon red pepper flakes, or to taste 1 pinch salt, or to taste 1 pinch ground black pepper, or to taste place olive oil, rice wine vinegar, orange zest, orange juice, dijon mustard, cumin, and red pepper flakes into a jar with a screw top; season with salt and black pepper. cover jar; shake vigorously to form a temporary emulsion, about 2 minutes.
ingredients_raw: ⅓ cup olive oil, or to taste
1 teaspoon ground cumin, or to taste
¼ teaspoon red pepper flakes, or to taste
1 pinch ground black pepper, or to taste
directions_raw: Place olive oil, rice wine vinegar, orange zest, orange juice, Dijon mustard, cumin, and red pepper flakes into a jar with a screw top; season with salt and black pepper.
Cover jar; shake vigorously to form a temporary emulsion, about 2 minutes.

recipe_title: Cherry Farro Salad with Sweet Vinaigrette
description: Here's a delicious cherry farro salad with a sweet vinaigrette recipe using a whole grain that tastes good and is also good for you! The flavors are refreshing and sweet while the crunchy nuts and farro give it wonderful texture!
directions: Whisk oil, maple syrup, vinegar, sugar, and salt together in a bowl until vinaigrette is smooth.
Cook and stir walnuts in a small skillet over low heat until toasted and fragrant, 3 to 4 minutes.

Bring chicken broth, farro, oregano, and basil to a boil in a saucepan. Reduce heat to medium-low; simmer for 10 minutes. Remove saucepan from heat, cover, and let sit until all liquid has been absorbed, about 30 minutes. Transfer farro to a glass bowl; cool to room temperature, about 30 minutes.
Stir walnuts, green apples, dried cherries, and parsley into farro; drizzle in vinaigrette and toss to coat. Refrigerate until chilled, at least 30 minutes.
ingredient_text: ¼ extra-virgin olive oil ¼ pure maple syrup ¼ apple cider vinegar white sugar ½ salt
directions_text: whisk oil, maple syrup, vinegar, sugar, and salt together in a bowl until vinaigrette is smooth. cook and stir walnuts in a small skillet over low heat until toasted and fragrant, 3 to 4 minutes. bring chicken broth, farro, oregano, and basil to a boil in a saucepan. reduce heat to medium-low; simmer for 10 minutes. remove saucepan from heat, cover, and let sit until all liquid has been absorbed, about 30 minutes. transfer farro to a glass bowl; cool to room temperature, about 30 minutes. stir walnuts, green apples, dried cherries, and parsley into farro; drizzle in vinaigrette and toss to coat. refrigerate until chilled, at least 30 minutes.
combined_text: cherry farro salad with sweet vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil ¼ cup pure maple syrup ¼ cup apple cider vinegar 2 teaspoons white sugar ½ teaspoon salt whisk oil, maple syrup, vinegar, sugar, and salt together in a bowl until vinaigrette is smooth. cook and stir walnuts in a small skillet over low heat until toasted and fragrant, 3 to 4 minutes. bring chicken broth, farro, oregano, and basil to a boil in a saucepan. reduce heat to medium-low; simmer for 10 minutes. remove saucepan from heat, cover, and let sit until all liquid has been absorbed, about 30 minutes. transfer farro to a glass bowl; cool to room temperature, about 30 minutes. stir walnuts, green apples, dried cherries, and parsley into farro; drizzle in vinaigrette and toss to coat. refrigerate until chilled, at least 30 minutes.
directions_raw: Whisk oil, maple syrup, vinegar, sugar, and salt together in a bowl until vinaigrette is smooth.
Cook and stir walnuts in a small skillet over low heat until toasted and fragrant, 3 to 4 minutes.

Bring chicken broth, farro, oregano, and basil to a boil in a saucepan. Reduce heat to medium-low; simmer for 10 minutes. Remove saucepan from heat, cover, and let sit until all liquid has been absorbed, about 30 minutes. Transfer farro to a glass bowl; cool to room temperature, about 30 minutes.
Stir walnuts, green apples, dried cherries, and parsley into farro; drizzle in vinaigrette and toss to coat. Refrigerate until chilled, at least 30 minutes.

description: This beautiful salad has a fresh cranberry dressing. It's a great way to start Thanksgiving dinner that is both festive and delicious.
ingredients: 2 heads romaine lettuce - rinsed, dried, and torn into bite-size pieces
2 medium heads Belgian endive - washed, dried and chopped
directions: Combine vinegar and cranberries in a saucepan; cook over medium heat until cranberries soften. Remove from heat; add olive oil, sugar, salt, and pepper. Place in blender and mix until smooth. Refrigerate until chilled.
Core and julienne one pear, core and dice the other.
Combine Romaine lettuce, endive, diced pears, walnuts, and Gorgonzola in a large bowl. drizzle and toss with enough dressing to coat.
Divide among salad plates and garnish with julienned pear. Top with additional walnuts if you like.
ingredient_text: ½ cider vinegar ¼ fresh cranberries ¼ olive oil white sugar ⅛ kosher salt pinch freshly ground black pepper heads romaine lettuce - rinsed dried torn into bite-size pieces medium heads belgian endive - washed dried chopped red anjou pears ½ toasted walnuts chopped ½ crumbled gorgonzola cheese
directions_text: combine vinegar and cranberries in a saucepan; cook over medium heat until cranberries soften. remove from heat; add olive oil, sugar, salt, and pepper. place in blender and mix until smooth. refrigerate until chilled. core and julienne one pear, core and dice the other. combine romaine lettuce, endive, diced pears, walnuts, and gorgonzola in a large bowl. drizzle and toss with enough dressing to coat. divide among salad plates and garnish with julienned pear. top with additional walnuts if you like.

ingredients_raw: 2 heads romaine lettuce - rinsed, dried, and torn into bite-size pieces
2 medium heads Belgian endive - washed, dried and chopped
directions_raw: Combine vinegar and cranberries in a saucepan; cook over medium heat until cranberries soften. Remove from heat; add olive oil, sugar, salt, and pepper. Place in blender and mix until smooth. Refrigerate until chilled.
Core and julienne one pear, core and dice the other.
Combine Romaine lettuce, endive, diced pears, walnuts, and Gorgonzola in a large bowl. drizzle and toss with enough dressing to coat.
Divide among salad plates and garnish with julienned pear. Top with additional walnuts if you like.

medium heads belgian endive - washed dried chopped

description: Tamarind lends a rich flavor, with notes of raisin and dried fruit, to this versatile vinaigrette. This dressing is fantastic on mango and shrimp salad, Asian chicken salad, or a simple arugula salad.
directions: Combine rice vinegar, tamarind concentrate, maple syrup, shallot, salt, and pepper in the bowl of a small food processor or blender. Pulse to combine.
With the unit running, stream in the oil a bit at a time, until thoroughly emulsified.
ingredient_text: ¼ rice vinegar tamarind concentrate pure maple syrup minced shallot ¼ salt ⅛ black pepper ⅓ extra-virgin olive oil
directions_text: combine rice vinegar, tamarind concentrate, maple syrup, shallot, salt, and pepper in the bowl of a small food processor or blender. pulse to combine. with the unit running, stream in the oil a bit at a time, until thoroughly emulsified.
combined_text: tamarind vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing ¼ cup rice vinegar 2 tablespoons tamarind concentrate 1 tablespoon pure maple syrup 1 tablespoon minced shallot ¼ teaspoon salt ⅛ teaspoon black pepper ⅓ cup extra-virgin olive oil combine rice vinegar, tamarind concentrate, maple syrup, shallot, salt, and pepper in the bowl of a small food processor or blender. pulse to combine. with the unit running, stream in the oil a bit at a time, until thoroughly emulsified.
directions_raw: Combine rice vinegar, tamarind concentrate, maple syrup, shallot, salt, and pepper in the bowl of a small food processor or blender. Pulse to combine.
With the unit running, stream in the oil a bit at a time, until thoroughly emulsified.

description: This tangy and flavorful dressing has a smooth 'zip' to it because of nutritional yeast and garlic powder. It's a great dressing for salads of any kind. It works great as a dipping sauce as well. Anyone who is looking for something different will definitely enjoy this dressing. The best part is that it takes only minutes to make. Vegetarians will love it for its protein packed, light, clean taste.
directions: Blend olive oil, nutritional yeast, tamari, cider vinegar, lemon juice, garlic powder, basil, oregano, and thyme together in a food processor or blender until dressing is smooth.
ingredient_text: olive oil nutritional yeast ⅔ tamari gluten-free soy sauce ½ cider vinegar ½ lemon juice garlic powder chopped fresh basil chopped fresh oregano chopped fresh thyme
directions_text: blend olive oil, nutritional yeast, tamari, cider vinegar, lemon juice, garlic powder, basil, oregano, and thyme together in a food processor or blender until dressing is smooth.
combined_text: tangy tamari dressing fruit salads fruit dressing 1 cup olive oil 1 cup nutritional yeast ⅔ cup tamari (gluten-free soy sauce) ½ cup cider vinegar ½ cup lemon juice 2 tablespoons garlic powder 1 tablespoon chopped fresh basil 1 tablespoon chopped fresh oregano 1 tablespoon chopped fresh thyme blend olive oil, nutritional yeast, tamari, cider vinegar, lemon juice, garlic powder, basil, oregano, and thyme together in a food processor or blender until dressing is smooth.
directions_raw: Blend olive oil, nutritional yeast, tamari, cider vinegar, lemon juice, garlic powder, basil, oregano, and thyme together in a food processor or blender until dressing is smooth.

description: Arugula is a delicious alternative to lettuce, and the citrus juice dressing is a delicious alternative to heavier store bought dressings. A lovely precursor to most meals, or a great stand-alone in the summer. Garnish with walnuts or sunflower seeds, if you like.
ingredients: ⅓ cup freshly squeezed grapefruit juice
⅓ cup freshly squeezed orange juice
⅓ cup extra virgin olive oil
6 ounces arugula - rinsed, dried and torn
directions: In a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine grapefruit juice, orange juice, olive oil, and salt. Shake vigorously.
In a salad bowl, combine arugula, pear, and red pepper. Drizzle dressing over salad, and toss to coat. Serve immediately.
ingredient_text: ⅓ freshly squeezed grapefruit juice ⅓ freshly squeezed orange juice ⅓ extra virgin olive oil salt taste ounces arugula - rinsed dried torn pear cored sliced red bell pepper thinly sliced
directions_text: in a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine grapefruit juice, orange juice, olive oil, and salt. shake vigorously. in a salad bowl, combine arugula, pear, and red pepper. drizzle dressing over salad, and toss to coat. serve immediately.
combined_text: arugula salad with citrus vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing ⅓ cup freshly squeezed grapefruit juice ⅓ cup freshly squeezed orange juice ⅓ cup extra virgin olive oil salt to taste 6 ounces arugula - rinsed, dried and torn 1 pear, cored and sliced 1 red bell pepper, thinly sliced in a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine grapefruit juice, orange juice, olive oil, and salt. shake vigorously. in a salad bowl, combine arugula, pear, and red pepper. drizzle dressing over salad, and toss to coat. serve immediately.

⅓ cup freshly squeezed orange juice
⅓ cup extra virgin olive oil
6 ounces arugula - rinsed, dried and torn
directions_raw: In a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine grapefruit juice, orange juice, olive oil, and salt. Shake vigorously.
In a salad bowl, combine arugula, pear, and red pepper. Drizzle dressing over salad, and toss to coat. Serve immediately.

description: A sweet and tangy dressing, perfect for any salad. I enjoy it on a spring mix with mixed berries, chopped apples, and toasted almond slices.
directions: Muddle together blueberries, strawberries, and raspberries with sugar in a medium bowl until combined.
Pour berry mixture into a blender and add balsamic vinegar, olive oil, Dijon, lemon juice, and honey. Blend until smooth.
ingredient_text: ½ fresh blueberries ½ fresh strawberries stemmed ½ fresh raspberries white sugar ¼ balsamic vinegar ¼ extra-virgin olive oil dijon mustard lemon juice honey
directions_text: muddle together blueberries, strawberries, and raspberries with sugar in a medium bowl until combined. pour berry mixture into a blender and add balsamic vinegar, olive oil, dijon, lemon juice, and honey. blend until smooth.
combined_text: mixed berry balsamic vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing ½ cup fresh blueberries ½ cup fresh strawberries, stemmed ½ cup fresh raspberries 1 teaspoon white sugar ¼ cup balsamic vinegar ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil 2 tablespoons dijon mustard 2 tablespoons lemon juice 2 tablespoons honey muddle together blueberries, strawberries, and raspberries with sugar in a medium bowl until combined. pour berry mixture into a blender and add balsamic vinegar, olive oil, dijon, lemon juice, and honey. blend until smooth.
directions_raw: Muddle together blueberries, strawberries, and raspberries with sugar in a medium bowl until combined.
Pour berry mixture into a blender and add balsamic vinegar, olive oil, Dijon, lemon juice, and honey. Blend until smooth.

description: This is an easy and delicious homemade salad dressing. Keeps for a day in the refrigerator. Great on a salad of spinach leaves, red onion, cherry tomatoes, and toasted sliced almonds.
ingredients: 1 clove garlic, chopped, or more to taste
directions: Press raspberries through a fine-mesh sieve using a spoon to remove seeds.

Whisk raspberries, rice vinegar, garlic, sugar, sea salt, and white pepper together in a bowl. Slowly drizzle avocado oil into raspberry mixture, whisking rapidly, until dressing is thick and creamy. Let sit until flavors develop, about 10 minutes.
ingredient_text: ¼ fresh raspberries rice vinegar lemon juice clove garlic chopped more taste ½ white sugar pinch sea salt pinch ground white pepper avocado oil
directions_text: press raspberries through a fine-mesh sieve using a spoon to remove seeds. whisk raspberries, rice vinegar, garlic, sugar, sea salt, and white pepper together in a bowl. slowly drizzle avocado oil into raspberry mixture, whisking rapidly, until dressing is thick and creamy. let sit until flavors develop, about 10 minutes.
combined_text: raspberry vinaigrette salad dressing fruit salads fruit dressing ¼ cup fresh raspberries 2 tablespoons rice vinegar 1 tablespoon lemon juice 1 clove garlic, chopped, or more to taste ½ teaspoon white sugar 1 pinch sea salt 1 pinch ground white pepper 6 tablespoons avocado oil press raspberries through a fine-mesh sieve using a spoon to remove seeds. whisk raspberries, rice vinegar, garlic, sugar, sea salt, and white pepper together in a bowl. slowly drizzle avocado oil into raspberry mixture, whisking rapidly, until dressing is thick and creamy. let sit until flavors develop, about 10 minutes.
ingredients_raw: 1 clove garlic, chopped, or more to taste
directions_raw: Press raspberries through a fine-mesh sieve using a spoon to remove seeds.

Whisk raspberries, rice vinegar, garlic, sugar, sea salt, and white pepper together in a bowl. Slowly drizzle avocado oil into raspberry mixture, whisking rapidly, until dressing is thick and creamy. Let sit until flavors develop, about 10 minutes.

description: Whenever I go to an Asian restaurant, I love the house salad dressing. This recipe was inspired by that, but it is not an exact copy. It also is NOT an authentic Asian recipe.
ingredients: ¼ cup minced onion, or more to taste
directions: Put yogurt, lemon juice, rice wine vinegar, ketchup, soy sauce, onion, garlic, ginger paste, sugar, and black pepper in a blender; blend on High until smooth.
Pour dressing into a sealable container, seal, and refrigerate at least 30 minute before serving.
ingredient_text: ¾ plain yogurt ¼ lemon juice ¼ rice wine vinegar ¼ ketchup ¼ soy sauce ¼ minced onion more taste cloves garlic minced ginger paste white sugar ground black pepper
directions_text: put yogurt, lemon juice, rice wine vinegar, ketchup, soy sauce, onion, garlic, ginger paste, sugar, and black pepper in a blender; blend on high until smooth. pour dressing into a sealable container, seal, and refrigerate at least 30 minute before serving.

Pour dressing into a sealable container, seal, and refrigerate at least 30 minute before serving.

recipe_title: Jicama and Pineapple Salad in a Cilantro Vinaigrette
description: Sweet and tangy work well together in this palate-pleasing salad.
ingredients: ½ fresh pineapple - peeled, cored, and cut into chunks
directions: Whisk together the serrano pepper, lime juice, rice vinegar, cilantro, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. Slowly drizzle in the olive oil while continually whisking. Add the pineapple and jicama; toss to coat. Allow to sit 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Place the spring mix in a large salad bowl; scatter the avocado over the lettuce; top with the marinated pineapple and jicama, drizzling the remaining vinaigrette over the salad. Serve NOW!
ingredient_text: serrano peppers seeded minced fresh lime juice rice vinegar bunch fresh cilantro minced ½ salt ¼ ground black pepper ¼ olive oil ½ fresh pineapple - peeled cored cut into chunks jicama peeled julienned mixed baby greens avocado - peeled pitted diced
directions_text: whisk together the serrano pepper, lime juice, rice vinegar, cilantro, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. slowly drizzle in the olive oil while continually whisking. add the pineapple and jicama; toss to coat. allow to sit 30 minutes to 1 hour. place the spring mix in a large salad bowl; scatter the avocado over the lettuce; top with the marinated pineapple and jicama, drizzling the remaining vinaigrette over the salad. serve now!

directions_raw: Whisk together the serrano pepper, lime juice, rice vinegar, cilantro, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. Slowly drizzle in the olive oil while continually whisking. Add the pineapple and jicama; toss to coat. Allow to sit 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Place the spring mix in a large salad bowl; scatter the avocado over the lettuce; top with the marinated pineapple and jicama, drizzling the remaining vinaigrette over the salad. Serve NOW!
ingredients_canonical: ½ fresh pineapple - peeled cored cut into chunks

description: This sweet and tart vinaigrette has an added spicy smokiness from the chipotle chile powder and an earthiness from the cumin. The combination of flavors is delicious on any salad or in a marinade.
ingredients: ½ teaspoon ground chipotle pepper, or to taste
directions: Whisk olive oil, sugar, lime juice, cumin, chipotle pepper, and garlic powder together in a bowl. Refrigerate until serving.
ingredient_text: ½ olive oil ¼ white sugar ¼ lime juice ½ ground cumin ½ ground chipotle pepper taste ¼ garlic powder
directions_text: whisk olive oil, sugar, lime juice, cumin, chipotle pepper, and garlic powder together in a bowl. refrigerate until serving.
combined_text: chipotle-lime vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing ½ cup olive oil ¼ cup white sugar ¼ cup lime juice ½ teaspoon ground cumin ½ teaspoon ground chipotle pepper, or to taste ¼ teaspoon garlic powder whisk olive oil, sugar, lime juice, cumin, chipotle pepper, and garlic powder together in a bowl. refrigerate until serving.
ingredients_raw: ½ teaspoon ground chipotle pepper, or to taste
directions_raw: Whisk olive oil, sugar, lime juice, cumin, chipotle pepper, and garlic powder together in a bowl. Refrigerate until serving.

description: I created this creamy cashew salad dressing recipe for my cousin who cannot eat (drum roll) dairy, soy, eggs, peanuts, or yeast. She wanted a creamy salad dressing, and after looking at others for inspiration, this is what we came up with. It is really nice, reminiscent of hummus, and pretty darn healthy! Please send notes and improvements!
ingredients: ½ cup water, or more as needed
salt and ground black pepper to taste
directions: Combine cashews, water, onion, olive oil, lime juice, garlic, basil, salt, and black pepper in the bowl of a food processor or blender; blend until completely smooth, 3 to 5 minutes. Add a little more water for a runnier dressing.
ingredient_text: ½ unsalted raw cashews ½ water more as needed ¼ onion olive oil lime juiced cloves garlic peeled ½ dried basil salt ground black pepper taste
directions_text: combine cashews, water, onion, olive oil, lime juice, garlic, basil, salt, and black pepper in the bowl of a food processor or blender; blend until completely smooth, 3 to 5 minutes. add a little more water for a runnier dressing.
combined_text: creamy cashew salad dressing fruit salads fruit dressing ½ cup unsalted raw cashews ½ cup water, or more as needed ¼ onion 2 tablespoons olive oil 1 lime, juiced 2 cloves garlic, peeled ½ teaspoon dried basil salt and ground black pepper to taste combine cashews, water, onion, olive oil, lime juice, garlic, basil, salt, and black pepper in the bowl of a food processor or blender; blend until completely smooth, 3 to 5 minutes. add a little more water for a runnier dressing.
ingredients_raw: ½ cup water, or more as needed
salt and ground black pepper to taste
directions_raw: Combine cashews, water, onion, olive oil, lime juice, garlic, basil, salt, and black pepper in the bowl of a food processor or blender; blend until completely smooth, 3 to 5 minutes. Add a little more water for a runnier dressing.

description: Homemade vinaigrette with mustard, balsamic vinegar and a touch of lemon is perfect over fresh salad greens and your choice of other add-ins like cheese, nuts, or fruit.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon Maille® Old Style Mustard or Honey Dijon Mustard, or more to taste
2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar, or more to taste
directions: Mix Maille® Old Style or Honey Dijon Mustard, balsamic vinegar, lemon juice and a pinch of salt until well blended.
Pour in oil, little by little, and mix until well blended. Add salt and pepper to taste. Serve over your favorite salad mix. Add a tasty cheese, some pecans, grapes or apple slices.

recipe_title: Chicken Mango Salsa Salad with Chipotle Lime Vinaigrette
description: A delicious, colorful, sweet and spicy salad with diced chicken, a mango pico de gallo salsa, blue cheese, and cranberries in a wonderful chipotle lime vinaigrette. It's almost too pretty to eat...almost.

directions: Mix mango, tomatoes, onion, jalapeno pepper, cilantro, and juice of 1 lime in a large bowl; set aside.
Whisk olive oil, 1/4 cup lime juice, sugar, chipotle chile powder, cumin, and garlic powder together in a bowl; set aside.
Toss spinach leaves, broccoli coleslaw mix, chicken, red, green, and yellow bell peppers, cranberries, and pecans together in a large bowl.
Spread mango salsa and blue cheese on top.
Drizzle lime dressing over salad and toss before serving.

recipe_title: Three Greens and Fruit with Vinaigrette
description: Use Bosc pears in this crisp green salad. It's great to serve during the cool weather months, and is a beautiful addition to any holiday table!
ingredients: ¾ teaspoon ground black pepper to taste
directions: In a bowl, mix the vinegar, oil, onion, sugar, salt, hot sauce, and pepper. Cover and refrigerate 1 hour.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook the walnuts, stirring constantly, until lightly toasted.
In a large bowl, mix the walnuts, romaine lettuce, butter lettuce, spinach, and pears. Toss with the dressing mixture to coat. Sprinkle with feta cheese to serve.

description: If you're a huge Caesar salad fan like I am, you may have had trouble finding a plant-based dressing that compared to the creamy, garlicky, dairy-based dressings everyone knows and loves. Over the past 2 years I have tweaked this to be just right. Went vegan and thought you'd never have a tasty Caesar dressing again? You will be pleasantly surprised! Oil-free, totally raw (except the capers), and packed with all the health benefits of cashews! Store in a mason jar in the fridge for up to 7 days.

description: Poached pears served with a red wine vinaigrette.
ingredients: 6 medium Bosc pears, peeled with stems intact
salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
directions: Trim flat the bottoms of pears, and stand them upright in the bottom of a large stock pot. Pour the wine and water into the pot, and turn the flame to high heat. Sprinkle with sugar, and add vanilla bean, star anise, cinnamon stick, and lemon and orange peels. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium low, and simmer 20 to 25 minutes. Remove pot from stove, and let cool completely. Reserve 1/4 cup poaching liquid. Transfer pears to a bowl, cover, and refrigerate.

Place 2 poached pears in a blender or food processor, and puree. Remove to a large bowl. Whisk in walnut oil, red wine vinegar, and reserved poaching liquid. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Cover, and refrigerate.
Place pears in individual bowls, and top with sauce.
ingredient_text: medium bosc pears peeled with stems intact white wine water sugar ½ vanilla bean halved lengthwise whole star anise pods small cinnamon stick lemon zested orange zested ¼ walnut oil ¼ red wine vinegar salt freshly ground black pepper taste
directions_text: trim flat the bottoms of pears, and stand them upright in the bottom of a large stock pot. pour the wine and water into the pot, and turn the flame to high heat. sprinkle with sugar, and add vanilla bean, star anise, cinnamon stick, and lemon and orange peels. bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium low, and simmer 20 to 25 minutes. remove pot from stove, and let cool completely. reserve 1/4 cup poaching liquid. transfer pears to a bowl, cover, and refrigerate. place 2 poached pears in a blender or food processor, and puree. remove to a large bowl. whisk in walnut oil, red wine vinegar, and reserved poaching liquid. season with salt and pepper to taste. cover, and refrigerate. place pears in individual bowls, and top with sauce.
combined_text: poached pears with wine vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing 6 medium bosc pears, peeled with stems intact 1 cup white wine 2 cups water 2 tablespoons sugar ½ vanilla bean, halved lengthwise 2 whole star anise pods 1 small cinnamon stick 1 lemon, zested 1 orange, zested ¼ cup walnut oil ¼ cup red wine vinegar salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste trim flat the bottoms of pears, and stand them upright in the bottom of a large stock pot. pour the wine and water into the pot, and turn the flame to high heat. sprinkle with sugar, and add vanilla bean, star anise, cinnamon stick, and lemon and orange peels. bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium low, and simmer 20 to 25 minutes. remove pot from stove, and let cool completely. reserve 1/4 cup poaching liquid. transfer pears to a bowl, cover, and refrigerate. place 2 poached pears in a blender or food processor, and puree. remove to a large bowl. whisk in walnut oil, red wine vinegar, and reserved poaching liquid. season with salt and pepper to taste. cover, and refrigerate. place pears in individual bowls, and top with sauce.
ingredients_raw: 6 medium Bosc pears, peeled with stems intact
salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
directions_raw: Trim flat the bottoms of pears, and stand them upright in the bottom of a large stock pot. Pour the wine and water into the pot, and turn the flame to high heat. Sprinkle with sugar, and add vanilla bean, star anise, cinnamon stick, and lemon and orange peels. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium low, and simmer 20 to 25 minutes. Remove pot from stove, and let cool completely. Reserve 1/4 cup poaching liquid. Transfer pears to a bowl, cover, and refrigerate.

Place 2 poached pears in a blender or food processor, and puree. Remove to a large bowl. Whisk in walnut oil, red wine vinegar, and reserved poaching liquid. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Cover, and refrigerate.
Place pears in individual bowls, and top with sauce.

salt freshly ground black pepper taste

recipe_title: Fresh Pear and Cherry Salad with Vanilla Pear Vinaigrette
description: A mix of juicy pears, sweetened nuts, dried cherries, and feta cheese makes this is a show-stopping sweet salad! The taste and colors will impress! You may add more of the pear syrup to the dressing to make it thinner, or increase the amount of yogurt for creamier results.
ingredients: 1 (15.25 ounce) can pears in light syrup, drained reserving syrup
¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
½ (10 ounce) package mixed salad greens
directions: Combine sugar, cinnamon and walnuts in a skillet over medium heat. Mix together until sugar and cinnamon are melted and walnuts are evenly coated. Remove from heat. Spread walnuts on a large plate to cool.
In the container of a blender, combine the drained pears, 1/3 cup of the reserved syrup from the can, vinegar, yogurt, honey, salt, pepper, vanilla extract, and nutmeg; blend until smooth.
Assemble the salad by tossing together the mixed greens, spinach, pear slices, dried cherries, feta cheese, and walnuts in a serving bowl. Serve with dressing on the side.

description: A simple, basic recipe for a versatile lemon dressing made with lemon curd that works well on almost any salad. Keeps nicely in the fridge.
directions: Blend lemon curd, cider vinegar, coconut oil, and garlic in a blender until well mixed.
ingredient_text: ounce jar lemon curd cider vinegar coconut oil ½ clove garlic crushed
directions_text: blend lemon curd, cider vinegar, coconut oil, and garlic in a blender until well mixed.
combined_text: easy lemon vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing 1 (10 ounce) jar lemon curd 1 cup cider vinegar 1 cup coconut oil ½ clove garlic, crushed blend lemon curd, cider vinegar, coconut oil, and garlic in a blender until well mixed.
directions_raw: Blend lemon curd, cider vinegar, coconut oil, and garlic in a blender until well mixed.

description: My dad's homemade dressing. Super easy and I haven't met a person who didn't like it yet! Great on iceberg salad cut into wedges (fun too!)
directions: Stir the sugar, vegetable oil, ketchup, vinegar, lemon juice, water, Worcestershire sauce, and minced onion together in a bowl until the sugar is dissolved and the ingredients are completely mixed. Chill in refrigerator 2 hours before serving.
ingredient_text: white sugar vegetable oil ketchup ¼ distilled white vinegar ¼ lemon juice ¼ water ¼ worcestershire sauce ¼ dried minced onion
directions_text: stir the sugar, vegetable oil, ketchup, vinegar, lemon juice, water, worcestershire sauce, and minced onion together in a bowl until the sugar is dissolved and the ingredients are completely mixed. chill in refrigerator 2 hours before serving.
combined_text: dad's prussian dressing fruit salads fruit dressing 1 cup white sugar 1 cup vegetable oil 1 cup ketchup ¼ cup distilled white vinegar ¼ cup lemon juice ¼ cup water ¼ cup worcestershire sauce ¼ cup dried minced onion stir the sugar, vegetable oil, ketchup, vinegar, lemon juice, water, worcestershire sauce, and minced onion together in a bowl until the sugar is dissolved and the ingredients are completely mixed. chill in refrigerator 2 hours before serving.
directions_raw: Stir the sugar, vegetable oil, ketchup, vinegar, lemon juice, water, Worcestershire sauce, and minced onion together in a bowl until the sugar is dissolved and the ingredients are completely mixed. Chill in refrigerator 2 hours before serving.

description: I got the idea for this dressing from my love for Post Cranberry and Almond Cereal - I thought it was a great flavor combination. I like this dressing over a mixed green salad, potato salad (yes, it actually works) and even grilled salmon or warm roast turkey breast and glazed ham steaks at Christmas. I really love the color of this dressing - whatever it touches looks so pretty.
ingredients: ½ teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes
directions: Whisk together the cranberry juice, lemon juice, cranberries, shallot, parsley, almonds, red pepper flakes, garlic powder, salt, and sugar in a small bowl until the sugar has dissolved. Whisk in the vegetable oil until the dressing is thick and smooth.
ingredient_text: ½ cranberry juice lemon juice dried cranberries finely chopped finely chopped shallot finely chopped fresh parsley ground almonds ½ crushed red pepper flakes ½ garlic powder salt white sugar ⅓ vegetable oil
directions_text: whisk together the cranberry juice, lemon juice, cranberries, shallot, parsley, almonds, red pepper flakes, garlic powder, salt, and sugar in a small bowl until the sugar has dissolved. whisk in the vegetable oil until the dressing is thick and smooth.
combined_text: cranberry-almond vinaigrette fruit salads fruit dressing ½ cup cranberry juice 1 tablespoon lemon juice 1 tablespoon dried cranberries, finely chopped 1 tablespoon finely chopped shallot 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh parsley 2 teaspoons ground almonds ½ teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes ½ teaspoon garlic powder 2 teaspoons salt 1 tablespoon white sugar ⅓ cup vegetable oil whisk together the cranberry juice, lemon juice, cranberries, shallot, parsley, almonds, red pepper flakes, garlic powder, salt, and sugar in a small bowl until the sugar has dissolved. whisk in the vegetable oil until the dressing is thick and smooth.

description: Shredded zucchini, green onions, and parsley are tossed with a lemon vinaigrette for a fabulous summer salad.
ingredients: 4 cups zucchini, julienned (1/4 x 1/4 x 2-inch strips)
1 ½ tablespoons Diamond Crystal® Kosher Salt
⅓ cup extra virgin olive oil
1 tablespoon white wine or rice vinegar
1 cup tightly packed fresh parsley leaves
1 ½ teaspoons freshly grated lemon zest
directions: Place zucchini strips in bowl; sprinkle with Diamond Crystal® Kosher Salt. Toss to mix. Let stand 20 minutes.
Combine olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, sugar (if using) and garlic in small bowl or small jar with tight-fitting lid. Whisk or shake to blend. Cover and refrigerate.
Rinse zucchini lightly with cold water; drain well.
Place zucchini on dish towel or several layers of paper towels. Shake or blot gently to remove excess moisture.
Place zucchini, parsley and sliced green onions in a bowl; toss gently to mix. Drizzle lightly with vinaigrette (reserving any extra vinaigrette); toss to mix.
Garnish with lemon zest and additional green onions, if desired.

description: This is my version of Waldorf salad. It's a very fast and easy recipe.
ingredients: 1 ½ tablespoons distilled white vinegar or to taste
directions: Whisk mayonnaise and vinegar together in a mixing bowl.
Add apples, celery, raisins, and walnuts. Season with salt and pepper; mix well.
Chill salad before serving, at least 30 minutes.
ingredient_text: ½ mayonnaise ½ distilled white vinegar taste apple - peeled cored chopped large stalk celery diced ½ raisins ½ chopped walnuts salt pepper taste
directions_text: whisk mayonnaise and vinegar together in a mixing bowl. add apples, celery, raisins, and walnuts. season with salt and pepper; mix well. chill salad before serving, at least 30 minutes.

description: This Waldorf salad with crisp chunks of apple, celery, and nuts is crunchy, sweet, and delicious. Sweet crisp apples, such as Granny Smith or Pink Lady work best. It's the perfect salad recipe for any time of the year and easy to vary the ingredients to suit your taste.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon white sugar or to taste
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Whisk mayonnaise, sugar, lemon juice, and salt together in a serving bowl.
Stir in apples, celery, walnuts, and raisins. Cover and chill in the refrigerator until ready to serve.
Serve and enjoy!
